name: Montage: Beat 17 — Lower Passages (Heit­folk Vent Repair)

text.content:
Beat 17 — Lower Passages (Heit­folk Vent Repair)

Act: Act 2 | Type: Montage Scene

Overview

The party descends into the Heitfolk lower passages to repair Rot-infected geothermal vent channels. This is the task Hildvarð Bruneson has set in exchange for safe passage through the tunnels. The passages are warm, forge-lit, and deeply old. Kaelen's ember-mark actively responds to the Rot-corruption here — she can purify it but it costs recovery test. Success determines how many warmth channels are restored and how long the Oasis heat lasts.

Kaelen mechanic: She spends 2 recovery test during this montage participating (the ember-mark suppresses Rot-corruption on contact). She cannot opt out if she wants the tunnels restored.

Running This Scene

This is a narrative montage — a series of challenges the party faces as a group. Rather than a single roll, play this out as a sequence of scenes where each character contributes.

GM Guidance

  • Go around the table and ask each player how their character is helping.
  • Call for ability checks as appropriate — suggested modified [Resolute←13] (moderate).
  • Let creative approaches succeed with good roleplaying, even without a roll.
  • Use the complications below as prompts — not every one needs to come up.
  • The goal is collaborative storytelling, not a pass/fail test.

Relevant Skills:

  • Strong
  • Quick or Discreet
  • Cunning or Cunning
  • Vigilant, Vigilant, or Vigilant
  • Persuasive, Persuasive, or Persuasive

Complications & Challenges

Use these as dramatic beats during the montage. Present them as situations the party must navigate together.

The first two vent channels are deep in the lower passages — ancient carved stone channels that normally carry hot water from the geothermal vents to the living areas above. Both are choked with Rot-corruption: black crystalline growths blocking the flow, Root-Runes on the walls screaming silently. Kaelen places her hands on the wall and the ember-mark blazes.

  • Reason: Analyze the channel blockage architecture — determine where the primary obstruction is to maximize Kaelen's purification efficiency. If successful, Kaelen needs only one contact point instead of three.
  • Might: Clear the physical crystalline Rot-growth from the channel mouth (it's brittle but requires force — safety goggles recommended; failure means shards).
  • Intuition: Sense which sections of Rot-growth are load-bearing for the corruption network vs. which are dormant. Destroying the wrong section triggers a collapse.
  • Presence: The Djúpdvergr workers watching are frightened of Kaelen's ember-mark. Keep the collaboration working — they need to trust her enough to hold the secondary channels open while she purifies.

Escalation

If the scene needs more tension, introduce these additional complications:

The third channel is the deepest — Hildvarð's son died in this section three years ago. The channel is still partially blocked by a collapse, not just Rot. It requires both physical clearing and purification simultaneously. Kaelen goes quiet when they pass through this section. Hildvarð, from the entrance: "Leave that one if it's too much."

  • Might: Clear the physical collapse debris (heavy stone — two heroes working in tandem have second chance (two rolls, take best)).
  • Reason: Identify the structural risk before clearing — removing the wrong stone brings down the ceiling.
  • Intuition: Sense whether the Rot in this channel is connected to a deeper source. If it is, purifying here alerts something. Determine whether to purify fully or just enough to restore flow.
  • Presence: Kaelen is visibly affected by this section (the memories in old stone reach her ember-mark). Keep her focused and functional while she works.

Outcomes

Based on how the party performed, use the following as a guide:

Total Success: Both channels cleared in Round 1. First warmth channel restored — warm water flows. The Djúpdvergr workers cheer quietly. Kaelen steadies herself against the wall (1 Hit Die spent).
Partial Success: One channel cleared. The second requires another approach. Kaelen spends 1 Hit Die. Party proceeds to Round 2 with the second channel still blocked.
Failure: A Rot-crystal collapse sends shards through the passage. Each hero takes 5 damage. The channel is sealed worse than before. Kaelen spends 1 Hit Die just stabilizing the section. Round 2 is harder.

Total Success: All three channels restored. Hildvarð: "Two years of warmth, maybe three. I didn't think I'd see this again." Kaelen spends her second Hit Die. Deep in the passage behind the cleared collapse, the party finds a small iron figure made by a child — warm to the touch, still. Hildvarð does not ask for it back.
Partial Success: Two channels fully restored; the third partially. Several months of warmth. Kaelen spends 1 Hit Die. The iron figure is found regardless.
Failure: Only one channel restored from Round 1. The collapse in the third channel cannot be safely cleared. Hildvarð accepts this quietly. Still provides the tunnels — the deal was help, not success. Kaelen emerges pale.
